feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to implement Value-Help in Input-Field for RAP OData Fiori/UI5 App?
entry.content:
I am developing a UI5 app that includes a SimpleForm with multiple input fields. Some of these fields require value help (F4 help) that fetches data from SAP tables in the backend.

From my understanding, RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) is primarily used for displaying lists in the frontend. However, in my case, I have a form with various input fields.

Do I maybe need to create a separate RAP-OData service with an EntitySet for each value Help Field? Or what would be the best practife for implementing value help in this scenario?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: error " file NOT found! "when doing a test service in ui5 application in sicf
entry.content:
Hello everyone!
I am having a problem with a sap ui5 fiori application.
I did a deploy-to-abap of a ui5 application in the DEV environment, then I go to the trx sicf and do a "test service" of the application and a window opens in the browser with the application.
The error occurred when I transported this application from DEV to QAS and then I went to the trx sicf and tried to do a "test service" of the application and the application opens blank in the browser with a text that says "file not found!
Do you know why this error can occur?
Or where I can see a trace of the error? I already tried from the trx /iwfnd/error_log and nothing appears.

Thanks

entry.title: Fiori check the odata entity/entity set
entry.content:
How to check or where can I find the odata entity/entity set from fiori app?

i checked the Inspect->Network tab and found a $batch and saw this URL in payload 
https://sample.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_SRV/$batch?sap-client=100

but i can't see the entity set used or if its using other DPC extension 

after clicking the button there should be an entity set like CREATE_ENTITYSET, CREATE_DEEP_ENTITYSET, etc?

entry.title: MIRO BADI using/Calling Fiori custom fields in (DRSEG_CI and CI_DRSEG_CUST)
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Implementing BADI MRM_ITEM_CUSTFIELDS, we can able to add custom tab and populate the custom fields appended in DRSEG_CI and CI_DRSEG_CUST.  

Since we are in S4 Migration and having Fiori custom fields created by SAP which are maintained in RSEG table, we would like to use the same custom fields for this MIRO enhancement, instead of creating a new one in DRSEG_CI and CI_DRSEG_CUST as it looks a duplicate and problem in syncing the posting data i.e., updating real time live data both in the Fiori custom fields and DRSEG_CI  custom fields. 

Please let me know any suitable solution to use the Fiori custom fields that are created in RSEG table.

@Sandra_Rossi : Need your help. Is it possible or not? Many Thanks.

Thanks in advance.

Kind regards.

entry.title: Excel Upload in Fiori Elements List Report Using Guided Development (Custom Action)
entry.content:
Hi, So recently i was trying to play around with the feature capabilities of SAP Fiori Tools in BAS (Guided Development). 
My goal was to  add a custom button on the table toolbar of a Fiori Elements List Report App, which pops up a dialog box containing a file uploader control and two buttons of 'ok' and 'cancel'.To achieve it i followed the following steps : - 

1. Created a custom action button named 'Upload Excel' using guided development.

2. Created an xml fragment file with a simple dialog and a fileuploader and invoked it from the extended controller.

And this is how it looks when we click on the 'Upload Excel' Button.

Now the problem is that my neither my endbutton(cancel) or begin button (upload) is working.
For example in this case, if I click on cancel, it should trigger the '

onDialogCancelPress' method and close the popup, right? But no boom, no response! , my method is not even getting triggered.
I think there is a problem with the controller reference, how do I use the correct context and get my method triggered?
Please help.

entry.title: Fiori List Report displays identical records despite underlying CDS View containing distinct data
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm facing an issue with my Fiori List Report App where it displays identical records, even though the underlying CDS View contains distinct data. I have verified that the data in the CDS View is correct, but the app still shows multiple rows with the same values for all fields. I suspect there might be a problem with data binding or the OData service configuration, but I haven't been able to pinpoint the exact cause.

As you can see on my screenshot from the CDS View, the data there is being read successfully from the database, but in the Fiori App it's not showing properly. Is there any annotation that I should add additionally for batching the data or what should I do so that it works?

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: How to restrict object page of standard sap fiori application and call custom URL ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to restrict object page of standard fiori application and call custom URL when click on any line item of a table.

By using adaptation project when we call the standard fiori app (app ID : F3893), we are not getting manifest.json file in the project structure to remove object page section and also trying to restrict object page navigation via extension controller, breakpoints are not triggered.

We are following below links.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/fiori-element-object-list-disable-navigation/qaq-p/12506350

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/8bd546e27a5f41cea6e251ba04534d70

Can some one help on this ?

entry.title: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile application through link in an email ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile application through link in an email.

Suppose based on some workflow configurations, you will get an email for sales order details.

Example : Sales order no : 1234

and some URL Link to open the relevant order in Standard Fiori application.

Generally, sap standard fiori apps are browser based URL.

Here the requirement is once they click on the link they want to see that sales order in mobile application not in the browser.

Earlier SAP Fiori client has one application was there, now its no longer available.

Now we have seen SAP Mobile Start 

Is it possible, to open that sales order directly in mobile application instead of browser ?

Thanks,

entry.title: How can I add several Simple Gantt Charts into a Simple Gantt Chart container in SAPUI5?
entry.content:
Hello,
 
I am trying to add multiple sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable dynamically into a Gantt Chart Container. I have defined the empty Gantt chart container in my XML view and I want to create multiple sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable in my JavaScript controller and add them to the Gantt chart container. 
 
 
I have the following code: 
        <gnt2:GanttChartContainer id="ganttContainer">
            <gnt2:toolbar>
<gnt2:ContainerToolbar id="toolbar1" design="Auto" showBirdEyeButton="true" showLegendButton="true" ganttSidePanel="onGanttSidePanel" 
                showSearchButton="false" showDisplayTypeButton="true"
 
                </gnt2:ContainerToolbar>
            </gnt2:toolbar>
                >
        </gnt2:GanttChartContainer>
 
JavaScript Controller:
 
 onInit: function () {
                var oGanttContainer = this.getView().byId("ganttContainer");
                var oGanttChart = new sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable(
                );
                oGanttContainer.addGanttChart(oGanttChart);
},
 
I get the following error: TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getRowMode')
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: e is null
    _updateRowHeightInExpandModel GanttChartWithTable-dbg.js:3632
 
If I try as follows, I get the same error:
var abc = new GanttChartWithTable({
enableChartOverflowToolbar: true
});
oGanttContainer.addGanttChart(oGanttChart);

Thanks for the help

entry.title: SAP Fiori Security Basics
entry.content:
Agenda

  • Introduction to SAP Fiori
  • Fiori Contents (Tiles, Catalogs, Groups, Spaces, Pages, Sections)
  • Fiori OData service, Target Mappings, Apps library
  • Fiori Deployments
  • Fiori Authorization and Security Activities
  • Fiori Role Design Approach
  • Fiori Troubleshooting

    Introduction to Fiori

    • SAP Fiori is a user interface technology that provides access to SAP system via apps. It’s an alternative to SAP GUI Logonpad.
    • Fiori interface is accessible via Fiori Launchpad (FLP) which is a web URL provided by Basis team (FLP is also opened when tcode /n/ui2/flp is executed).

      Fiori Tiles / Apps

      • A tile is a container that represents an app on the Fiori launchpad page. So, it’s used to display and launch app on the Fiori launchpad.
      • There are 3 types of Fiori apps: Transactional, Analytical, Factsheet

        –Transactional Apps: These apps let you perform transactional tasks, such as creating travel requests and approving purchase orders. They represent simplified views and interaction with existing business processes and solutions. They run best on an SAP HANA database but can also be ported to other databases with acceptable performance.

        –Analytical Apps: Analytical apps provide real-time analysis of business operations by displaying a large volume of data analytically. These apps run on an SAP HANA database.

        –Factsheets: These apps display contextual information and key facts about central objects used in your business operations. For example, you can navigate from a document to the related business partner or to the master data.

        Fiori Catalogs and Groups

        SAP Fiori apps are organized through catalogs and groups.

        • Catalog:

          –Catalog contains set of apps/tiles. Catalogs are assigned to roles. Users can browse through the list and choose the tile that they want to display on the entry page of SAP Fiori Launchpad.

          • Group:

            –A group is a subset of Applications from one or more catalogs. Which tiles are displayed on a user’s Home page depends on the groups assigned to the user’s role. In addition, the user can personalize the entry Fiori page by adding or removing Applications to pre-delivered groups or self-defined groups.

             Fiori Spaces, Pages and Sections

            Spaces and Pages is part of new layout design of Fiori launchpad which is called Fiori 3.0.

            • Space/Page contains subset of apps from one or more catalogs. Tiles are displayed on a user’s launchpad depending on the spaces assigned to the user’s role. User can personalize the pages by adding or removing tiles from assigned catalogs.
            • A space is represented as a navigation item in the top-level navigation, and a space can have one or more pages. A page consists of sections that are used to further structure the content. Sections are optional.

              Fiori OData service, Target Mappings, Apps Library

              • OData is a resource-based web protocol for querying and updating data. OData defines operations on resources using HTTP commands (for example, GET, PUT, POST, and DELETE). To provide users with the UI access to Apps, start authorization for the activated OData services used by apps must be granted. OData services are service type and access is given through Authorization default from PFCG menu and services are added to S_SERVICE authorization object. Frontend OData services are denoted as IWSG and IWSV is for backend OData services.
              • A tile contains the intent, which defines what action needs to be taken, such as Create a Sales Order, but to execute the intent, a navigation target is needed to launch the application. This is done with the help of another UI content entity known as target mapping. To launch an application, both tile and target mapping are needed.
              • SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library is the single point information to all SAP Fiori apps. You will need SAP Fiori apps reference library to configure standard Fiori apps. Following details are available in apps library necessary for security configuration for an app.

                –Required back-end system version

                –OData service

                –Target mapping

                –Technical / Business Catalog

                –Business Group

                –Business Role

                Fiori Deployments

                Before discussing Fiori deployments, let’s understand front-end and back-end servers.

                Front-end server is a system where Fiori components are installed whereas back-end server refers to the system that contains the business logics to fetch data from database.

                There are two deployment options for SAP Fiori for on-premise SAP systems: Central Hub and Embedded.

                Fiori Authorization

                • The end-user requires basic authorizations to start and use the SAP Fiori Launchpad. SAP delivers predefined role templates for end-users, as well as for administrators. The roles are:

                  -> SAP_UI2_USER_700 (new role SAP_FLP_USER) for end-users

                  -> SAP_UI2_ADMIN_700 (new role SAP_FLP_ADMIN) for administrators

                  • Custom roles must be built by copying above roles for user assignment. These table represents the OData services needed for users.
                  • Please note that both IWSG and IWSV services access must be given and FDM services are required to manage Fiori with Space/Page design.

                     

                    • To create space/page, admin needs access to below apps.

                      Fiori Security Activities (Creating a custom catalog)

                      Execute tcode /n/ui2/flpd_cust to open the Fiori launchpad designer and follow the steps as mentioned in below screenshot to create a custom catalog.

                      Fiori Security Activities (Adding a Fiori app in custom catalog)

                      Let’s understand how to add a Fiori app called ‘Create Inbound Delivery’ in custom catalog created earlier.

                      From Fiori Apps Library, we can find the Target Mapping and the standard catalog where this app exists.

entry.title: CDS view for SAP APP F4515
entry.content:
Hi All, 

We are currently developing a CDS view for the messages displayed in the Message Monitoring App (F4515). We would appreciate assistance in identifying the backend table that stores this data. Additionally, we need to know the CDS view for these fields to create a report based on them

Thanks 

YSN.

entry.title: SAP UI5 Annotations Advantages with Json Model
entry.content:
Caused by a few issues i need to convert my Odata V2 Model to Json Model in the OnInit function of my latest UI5 app. So far so good ... no problem so far.

But here comes my problem within this Json Model:
I want to use the automatic advantages of the Odata V2 Annotations within this Json Model Binding, f.e. the automatic search help dialogs and so on ... Is this somehow possible?

entry.title: How to put fiori app check just like SY-TCODE check in sap backend enhancement
entry.content:
In SAP Backend, when any customer exit/BADI is called from multiple tcodes then we can keep a check of SY-TCODE so that cusotmer exit code will be executed only in particular case.
Do we have similar field when calls are coming from standard fiori app? There is one customer exit getting called from 2 different fiori apps but need restriction for only 1 app.

entry.title: Display a Custom Message/Alert on opening of a Standard Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement to display a custom message/information/alert on opening of a standard Fiori app or on landing page of standard Fiori app.

Is it possible from Fiori Launchpad plugins?

How can this be achieved?

entry.title: How to pass dynamic field value in annotation @Consumption.filter.defaultValue
entry.content:
Hello,

I have requirement to display default value in selection field in FIORI element. For that I am using @Consumption.filter.defaultValue annotation. When I pass constant value in quote its working. But I want a dynamic value in selection field. How to pass dynamic field value in the following annotation @Consumption.filter.defaultValue.

Thanks

entry.title: Requirements for Upgrade to SAP_UI 7.57 on ECC6.0
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

our ECC 6.0 SAP-System is running on the following versions:
- SAP NETWEAVER 7.5
- EHP8 FOR SAP ERP 6.0
- SAP_GWFND 750
- SAP_BASIS 750
- SAP_ABA 750
- SAP_UI 754

We need some new SAP_UI5 features from version 1.108 and now we want to upgrade to this version.
Can anyone tell me which steps/versions/platforms we exactly need to go to use this 1.108 version?

I'm very confused from all the overview pages and i don't know what to do anymore.

Best Regards,
Chris

entry.title: Fiori custom column data
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Im studying fiori and im using BAS, I used a Fiori List Report Template and I found out that Annotations can be inserted from Open Guided Development

I want to concatenate the two columns and I want to add a new column not from odata/backend side but for frontend view only, 

my problem is

the generated files of BAS do not have a Controller and View file, where can I do some modification here to concatenate the two columns?

this is my annotation

entry.title: Is it possible to "consume" metadata extensions in SAPUI5
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I learn ABAP RAP and have defined a metadata extension in my service.

 

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI.headerInfo: {
  typeName: 'Handling Unit',
  title: { value: 'HandlingUnitNumber', type: #STANDARD },
  description: { value: 'HandlingUnitUsageDescription' }
}
annotate entity /#####/#####
  with 
{
  .facet: [{
    id: 'HeaderFacet',
    purpose: #HEADER,
    type: #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE,
    targetQualifier: 'HeaderItems'
  }]
  
  .fieldGroup: [{
    qualifier: 'HeaderItems',
    position: 10
  }]
  PackagingMaterialName;
   
}

 

Is it possible to use/call/consume this metadata extension inside a SAPUI5 app as an addition? I read something about extensions points, but they didn't help me much 😕

 

Any approach or hint is appreciated. 

 

Kind regards

Jürgen

entry.title: Blank spaces in custom UI5 master detail apps in a newly migrated S4HANA launchpad.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

One of our clients recently upgraded from ECC to the latest S4HANA on-premise system, and some of the sap ui5 apps now display a blank space between the title and list in a master page. This space disappears when I reload or minimize, but it reappears when I navigate from the home page.

I am getting the following depricated error messages in the console, but they are happening to all of my apps. If I need to update, how do I do it? These APIs are something SAP UI5 calls internally, not custom code.

I have already tried changing themes; this is not a theme-specific issue.

Changing the resource to a CDN link also failed. Please guide if there is some necessary steps needs to be done on custom ui5 apps and redeploy.

 

Thanks,

Aravind

entry.title: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Output management in S/4HANA

Introduction to output management

After working with many clients in different fields, I've noticed that most businesses struggle with output management. Each client has their own idea of what output means, whether it's PDF documents, forms, emails, or iDocs. While these views are somewhat correct, I think output management is about effectively communicating with customers or vendors using the right channels and documents.

When we talk about output management, I see it as combining "output" (like print, email, iDoc) with "management" (how we set it up to reuse the functionality). Output management in SAP S/4HANA is a big improvement in how businesses generate and distribute documents. This new framework solves the complexities and inefficiencies of the many old output solutions in previous SAP systems. This blog will mostly cover the output management features in on-premises S/4HANA.

Legacy Output Solutions

Historically, SAP provided several output management tools tailored to specific business areas:

  • SD Output Control (NAST): Used primarily for sales and distribution processes.
  • FI Correspondence: Handles financial document communications.
  • FI-CA Print Workbench: Manages print jobs for contract accounts receivable and payable.
  • CRM Post-Processing Framework (PPF): Deals with post-processing activities in customer relationship management.

While these tools were effective, they required customers to learn and maintain distinct frameworks for each business area. This led to a high total cost of ownership (TCO) and varied user experiences.

What is NAST

NAST is a central component in SAP for managing and controlling the output of documents. It is part of the SAP Output Control framework and is used primarily in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, but its functionality extends to other areas such as Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI).

Key features of NAST include:

  • Message control
  • Output type
  • Condition record
  • Processing routine
  • Transmission medium
  • Scheduling

NAST operates using the condition technique, which is the traditional method of working in SAP.

The Need for a Unified Solution

In SAP S/4HANA projects, the fragmentation of output management tools presented several challenges:

  1. Complexity: Customers had to navigate and master different frameworks for different tasks.
  2. Maintenance: Each framework required separate setup and ongoing maintenance.
  3. User Experience: Disparate tools resulted in inconsistent user experiences across the SAP ecosystem.

What is Output Management in S/4HANA?

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA is a comprehensive framework designed to streamline the creation, formatting, and distribution of business documents. It modernizes and centralizes outdated output mechanisms, effectively meeting the diverse needs of various business processes.

Key features of S/4HANA Output Management include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Offers a single point of configuration for all output types, reducing complexity and easing maintenance. This common setup point can be used across different modules.
  • Flexibility and Scalability: Supports various output channels and formats, making it adaptable to diverse business needs and scalable for future growth.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides consistent and intuitive user interfaces, improving efficiency and satisfaction across different modules.
  • Simplified Maintenance: Eliminates the need for complex master data maintenance and extensive cutover activities.
  • Advanced Output Channels: Supports modern output channels like email, print, fax, and electronic data interchange (EDI), in addition to traditional methods.
  • Improved Template Management: Utilizes advanced tools for designing and managing document templates, such as Adobe Forms, enhancing document consistency and branding.

Different between NAST and Output management in S/4HAN

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

NAST is the traditional output control functionality used in earlier versions of SAP, such as SAP ECC. It manages the output of business documents through various channels like print, email, fax, and EDI. Key characteristics of NAST include:

  • Decentralized Configuration: Each application module (e.g., SD, MM, FI) has its own configuration, leading to complexity and redundancy.
  • Limited Flexibility: Customization and enhancement of output processes require significant effort and often involve custom ABAP development.
  • Condition Records: Utilizes condition records to determine when and how outputs are triggered.
  • Processing Routines: Depends on traditional forms like SAP script and Smart Forms for document formatting.
  • Separate Handling: Each business area (e.g., sales orders, invoices) has its own output determination and processing logic
  • It also covers Multiple Output Mediums and Error Handling and Monitoring

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

The new Output Management framework in SAP S/4HANA is designed to overcome the limitations of NAST and provide a more streamlined and efficient approach. Its key features include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Provides a unified setup for all output types, reducing complexity and maintenance efforts.
  • Enhanced Flexibility: Supports various output channels and formats with easier customization options.
  • Advanced Template Management: Uses Adobe Forms for designing and managing document templates, offering more sophisticated formatting and branding capabilities.
  • Output Parameter Determination: Utilizes a modern method for determining output parameters based on business context, simplifying the output determination process.
  • Improved User Experience: Consistent and user-friendly interfaces across different modules enhance operational efficiency.
  • Integration: Seamlessly integrates with other SAP S/4HANA components and external systems, improving overall document management and processing.

Key Differences

Feature

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Configuration

Decentralized, specific to each module

Centralized, unified for all output types

Flexibility

Limited, requires custom ABAP development

Enhanced, easier customization

Forms and Templates

SAP script, Smart Forms

Adobe Forms

Output Parameter Determination

Condition records

Modern, context-based parameter determination

User Experience

Inconsistent across modules

Consistent and user-friendly

Integration

Separate handling for each business area

Seamless integration with S/4HANA components

Maintenance

Complex, higher total cost of ownership (TCO)

Simplified, lower TCO

 

Advantages and features of Output management

SAP S/4 HANA Output Management has the below features

  • Operates seamlessly in both cloud and on-premises environments.
  • Offers a unified solution for all output related tasks across the product and modules
    Reduce master data maintenance and relatively less time consuming for activation
  • Supports standard extensibility for configuration documents
  • Offers central monitoring tools for key users, enhancing oversight and control.

Based on Business Rule Framework (BRF), offering greater flexibility in managing output processes.

How it works?

For determining output types in transactions, we can utilize Output Parameter Determination (OPD). Maintenance of OPD can function similarly to master data or be set up via transport management. I recommend using OPD as a client-specific change. Using transports can lead to confusion, especially if other rollouts are occurring simultaneously.

Prerequisites –

There are few back-end changes you need to do it before maintenance of OPD setup.

  • Check if your application object type is active
  • Check if your Output type is available and if you want to use your own output then create new one by doing copy of standard output type.
  • Assign channel to your Output type.
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type
  • Assign Email template to output type.

To use Output management, we need to activate the required object and if we activate Output management then system will redirect output always to Output management and not to NAST.

Main parameters in OPD (Output Parameter Determination)

Below are the key parameters,

Output management is BRF based functionality which can be setup by using below 7 steps in the SAP using OPD.

Let’s check in the system –

OPD (Output parameter determination) screen

There are multiple steps (as per above screenshot) which should be maintained to determine output in billing document as per this example, here we will take billing output type as an example.

 

  1. Output Type –

This step begins by deciding whether to process the output immediately or later via a job. As shown below, for the combination of billing type F2 and output type BILLING_DOCUMENT, we can choose to process the output type immediately or through a background job (VF31F) at the time of billing document creation. Additionally, in the billing document, we can manually add the output type and set the dispatch time manually.

 

 2. Receiver –

In this step, we can determine the output type for a specific role (partner function). If we have four lines with different roles and want to consider only the first two, we will mark the Exclusive indicator 'X' for the second line. This way, the system will only consider the first two entries and ignore any entries after the second line.

3. Channel –

In this stage, the 'Channel' will be defined. The channel could be IDoc, Print, Email, etc. This stage will also include an exclusive indicator.

For instance, if the exclusive indicator 'X' is set for IDoc as shown below, the system will not search for or determine the PRINT and EMAIL channels.

4. Printer setting –

In this phase, we can specify the printer and the number of copies to be printed after creating the document. Before setting up the printer in this phase, it is necessary to install the printer in SAP.

Additionally, please be aware that the new output management system supports Printer Spool Devices limited to PDF device types only (PDFUC: PDF Unicode 1.3 / PDF1: PDF ISO Latin1 4.60+).

This restriction is detailed under the Symptoms section in note 268480 and SAP Note. 2294198

5. Email setting –

In this phase, the sender's email ID can be configured. This means that when an email is sent to the customer, the sender's email will be set up here, and the recipient's email will be the customer's email address.

Additionally, we can also establish the email template in this phase. An email template specifies the format and content that the customer will receive from us via email.

6. Email recipient –

The email address (recipient address) retrieved in the billing document is sourced from BP master data or determined through this step.

If you have configured an email address here, this setting takes precedence.

Multiple email addresses can be maintained in this determination step by separating them with ';'.
You can also define the email type code with TO, CC, BCC.

7. Form Template –

This step is intended for maintaining the form template used for each output. Ensure that the form template maintained here has been assigned to the output type in SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Form Templates.

If the language is not specified in OPD, it will be retrieved from the customer master data of the output recipient.

8. Output Relevance –

This step verifies if the output item is flagged as relevant for output.

If the relevance indicator is not set to 'X - true', the output item determined in the billing document will not be considered relevant for output.

Consequently, its status will remain 'In preparation' in the billing document under the output section.

 

OPD functionalities –

Adding extra predefined standard fields

Adding new columns or fields: OPD provides default fields that cover all necessary steps we've discussed. However, often after client discussions, we realize additional parameters are needed to define our objects in OPD differently.

In OPD, there are additional fields available that we can add as columns on the main OPD screen. These can then be utilized in determination combinations as needed.

To do this, navigate to Edit -> Table settings -> Insert column -> From context data object -> Search.

Click on search

Following this step, a list of standard additional fields will be available for us to add and utilize in OPD. If the required field is still not available, we can proceed with an enhancement to add the necessary field to this list. This process requires the involvement of an ABAP specialist.

Simulation functionality

Having reviewed all seven steps, we can use them to determine the output type in the transaction.

To preview the output in simulation mode, use the highlighted option below. This allows us to simulate the output effectively.

Key points and learnings –

Pros

  • As OPD is BRF based functionality, we can import and export data using predefined template.
  • New column (fields) can be added and can be removed as per requirement.
  • SAP S/4HANA output management is recommended but not mandatory.
  • The scope of SAP S/4HANA output management is not comparable with the scope of NAST.
  • SAP S/4HANA output control is not designed to completely replace NAST.
  • Innovations will only be made in the new framework (SAP S/4HANA output management)

Cons –

  • It will only cover the output part of NAST (print, email, partly EDI).
  • The rest – like system integration, workflow, or special function – is not (and will not be) in the scope of SAP S/4HANA output control.
  • Customers will lose all NAST features when switching to SAP S/4HANA Output Control. Using S/4HANA Output Control for applications that previously relied on NAST is only advisable in exceptional cases
  • iDocs are not fully supported by the new output management. There are few restrictions as compared to NAST.
  • NO ALE is supported in Output management.
  • No tool is available for conversion from NAST to Output management.

Reference –

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13492419

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d7bd4b5d70d94f09bcf99b221a7a1688.html

  • SAP Note 2292539 - SAP S/4HANA output control – configuration
  • 2843926 - It is not possible to activate rules in App 'Output Parameter Determination' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • SAP Note 2248229 –SAP S/4HANA output control - download XML file
  • SAP Note 2292571 –SAP S/4HANA output control -technical setup 
  • SAP Note 2292646 –SAP S/4HANA output control -form templates with fragments 
  • SAP Note 2292681 –SAP S/4HANA output control –master form templates 
  • SAP Note 2294198 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized forms 
  • SAP Note 2367080 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized master forms
